requests.name: OWNER — TRIAL company — create checkout session → 201 + url
requests.docs:
PRE-REQUISITE: Signed in as OWNER. Company subscription_status=TRIAL. STRIPE_SECRET_KEY and STRIPE_PRICE_ID must be set in api/.env.

Expects 201 with { url: 'https://checkout.stripe.com/...' }. Open that URL in a browser to complete the Stripe test checkout.

requests.name: OWNER — LOCKED company — create checkout session → 201 + url
requests.docs:
PRE-REQUISITE: SQL: UPDATE "Company" SET subscription_status='LOCKED' WHERE id='<id>';

LOCKED companies can still subscribe. Expect 201 + checkout URL.

requests.name: ENGINEER — create checkout session → 403
requests.docs: PRE-REQUISITE: Signed in as ENGINEER role. RolesGuard should reject with 403. ENGINEER must never be able to start a checkout.

requests.name: ACTIVE company — create checkout session → 400

tests.value: Company already has an active subscription.

requests.docs:
PRE-REQUISITE: SQL: UPDATE "Company" SET subscription_status='ACTIVE' WHERE id='<id>';
Sign in as OWNER. Expect 400 with message 'Company already has an active subscription.'

requests.name: Unauthenticated — create checkout session → 401
requests.docs: No session cookie. SessionGuard should reject before BillingController is reached. Expect 401 (or 403 depending on SessionGuard behaviour).

requests.name: Webhook — invalid signature → 400
requests.docs: Sends a fake/invalid stripe-signature header. Stripe constructEvent should reject it and the endpoint must return 400. No session required — this route is @Public().

requests.name: ⚡ Stripe CLI — checkout.session.completed (read docs)
requests.docs:
USE STRIPE CLI — do not send this manually.

Local webhook testing with Stripe CLI:

1. Install: https://stripe.com/docs/stripe-cli
2. Login: stripe login
3. Forward events to local server:
   stripe listen --forward-to http://localhost:3000/api/billing/webhook
4. Copy the webhook signing secret from Stripe CLI output into api/.env:
   STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET=whsec_...
5. From the UI, click 'Subscribe for £59/month' as an OWNER.
6. Complete checkout in Stripe test mode (use card 4242 4242 4242 4242, any future date, any CVC).
7. Confirm Stripe CLI shows 'checkout.session.completed' forwarded with 200 response.
8. Run GET /api/companies/me — confirm subscription_status=ACTIVE.
9. Check api logs for: 'Company <id> activated after successful checkout'

Alternative: trigger test events:
   stripe trigger checkout.session.completed

requests.name: ⚡ Stripe CLI — customer.subscription.deleted (read docs)
requests.docs:
USE STRIPE CLI:

1. After completing checkout (company is ACTIVE with a stripe_subscription_id).
2. Cancel the subscription in Stripe test dashboard, or trigger:
   stripe trigger customer.subscription.deleted
3. Confirm api logs: 'Company <id> locked due to subscription deletion'
4. Run GET /api/companies/me — confirm subscription_status=LOCKED.
5. SubscriptionGuard will block all write requests for that company.

requests.name: ⚡ Stripe CLI — invoice.payment_failed (read docs)
requests.docs:
USE STRIPE CLI:

   stripe trigger invoice.payment_failed

Confirm api logs: 'payment_failed_at updated for company <id>'
The company is NOT immediately locked — subscription_status stays unchanged.
payment_failed_at is set to the current timestamp for future dunning/reminder logic (Step H).
